description_long: This bowl is shallow and wide, crafted from what appears to be a natural coconut shell, giving it a rustic and organic appearance. The surface is predominantly dark brown with lighter, irregular streaks and patches, characteristic of coconut shell texture. Four small, round drainage holes are present near one edge of the bowl, suggesting it may be used for draining or as a soap dish. The rim is uneven and slightly wavy, further emphasizing its handmade, natural origin. The interior and exterior both display the fibrous, marbled pattern typical of coconut shell, with a matte, non-glossy finish. The base is slightly raised, providing stability when placed on flat surfaces.
description: A shallow, wide bowl made from coconut shell, featuring a natural brown marbled texture and four drainage holes near the rim.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from above, highlighting its wide, shallow form, natural marbled pattern, and the four drainage holes near the edge.
description_view_1: This angled view emphasizes the bowl's uneven rim and the placement of the drainage holes, with more of the marbled pattern visible.
description_view_2: The bowl is shown from a lower angle, making the interior surface and the subtle curvature of the rim more apparent, with only two holes visible.
description_view_3: This view displays the bowl's interior depth and the organic, irregular edge, with the drainage holes partially visible.
attribute_context_of_use: draining small items or serving dry food
